content: It looks like support is going to be merged in 6.1: https://www.zdnet.com/article/linus-torvalds-rust-will-go-into-linux-6-1/
I don't trust the press, I trust Miguel's words ;-)
I'm trying not to count my chickens before they're hatched, but wow, it's incredible to be in eyesight of this milestone, reflecting back on the PyCon where it all started for me :-)

Miguel Ojeda said:

https://lwn.net/Articles/908320/

https://lwn.net/SubscriberLink/908320/5f1640d722bb2fa6/

This is a free link ;-)

Miguel Ojeda said:

In particular for Rust: https://lwn.net/Articles/908347/

Same again: https://lwn.net/SubscriberLink/908347/da67a5162d1bc4a3/

Finally, Gleixner admonished the Rust developers to not change the semantics of any C locking primitives; it's worth noting that they have shown no inclination to do that so far. Ts'o added that Rust's locking abstractions should be made to work with the lockdep locking checker from the beginning. Chris Mason interjected that, if lockdep is needed for Rust code, that will be another sign that the language has succeeded and it will be time to "do a victory dance".

Boqun Feng said:

Finally, Gleixner admonished the Rust developers to not change the semantics of any C locking primitives; it's worth noting that they have shown no inclination to do that so far. Ts'o added that Rust's locking abstractions should be made to work with the lockdep locking checker from the beginning. Chris Mason interjected that, if lockdep is needed for Rust code, that will be another sign that the language has succeeded and it will be time to "do a victory dance".

Is it a typo? Why "lockdep is needed for Rust" is a sign for success?
I think Chris meant that if people need tooling, then it means Rust is actually being used in production, thus a sign of success for Rust

It happened! Such an incredible milestone.

:heart: to everyone who made this happen.
Huge congratulations to everyone involved! It's an incredible milestone!

Wedson Almeida Filho said:

Champagne tomorrow? :)

I will fetch it :)
